--- Thread (48 messages, 2018-03-15) ---
[2018-03-15 04:18] http404error: I think a buildtime 2 drone variant would be a better place to start
[2018-03-15 04:18] f300xen (f0x): i mean, that's thorium dynamo
[2018-03-15 04:18] http404error: Compare Thorium Dynamo, which is quite good, but the buildtime 2 is a big downside
[2018-03-15 04:19] f300xen (f0x): dynamo is better than normal drones by a small amount usually, so...
[2018-03-15 04:19] http404error: But I think there's room for other "buildtime 2" economy
[2018-03-15 04:19] http404error: Thorium doesn't have to have a monopoly on it
[2018-03-15 04:19] http404error: You'd think the downside of Thorium would be that it can be rushed against, but in practice that doesn't really work...
[2018-03-15 04:20] http404error: So maybe something more fragile would be neat, so you have to consider defending it carefully
[2018-03-15 04:21] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): The 5EE version costs 8 over Build.
[2018-03-15 04:22] f300xen (f0x): 0-tech attack is a really bad idea anyways
[2018-03-15 04:22] http404error: I'm not really understanding you
[2018-03-15 04:23] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Pays 1:Gold: per :Time: passed.
[2018-03-15 04:23] http404error: Techless attack can work in some special cases but I'd make it the focus of the unit instead of something else tacked on
[2018-03-15 04:23] http404error: You know, you could just consume a Drone or something instead of having that weird upkeep cost
[2018-03-15 04:23] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Pay gold per build time tick isn't a thing, because there isn't an upkeep phase and you don't always float gold
[2018-03-15 04:24] http404error: That's just how Prismata does upkeeps, I would strongly recommend just doing that instead
[2018-03-15 04:24] supergreyman (Greyman): Did you know: Thorium is basically DDC but half the price and then doubling the build time.
[2018-03-15 04:24] http404error: Complex costs need to exist for a good reason; this one does not
[2018-03-15 04:25] http404error: I don't think anyone's submitted a multitech building yet!
[2018-03-15 04:25] http404error: There's only one in the game so far... technically I guess it doesn't change your turn 1 play though
[2018-03-15 04:26] http404error: maybe a drone/tech hybrid unit could be super good t1
[2018-03-15 04:26] http404error: trick is making it not broken of course
[2018-03-15 04:26] http404error: Typically the main way units cause you to break DD on turn 1 is by "being Vivid or Wild Drone"
[2018-03-15 04:26] supergreyman (Greyman): There's two actually.
[2018-03-15 04:27] supergreyman (Greyman): If you count Synthesizer.
[2018-03-15 04:27] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Cicada (1.3): Supply 4. Cost: 3:Gold: 1:Energy:. :Fragile:2. :Time:4. Turn: Get 2:Gold:, :Attack:.
[2018-03-15 04:27] http404error: Synth is really cool
[2018-03-15 04:28] http404error: That's a lot more interesting now, @StrShPlyr
[2018-03-15 04:28] http404error: I still think Buildtime 4 is excessive though
[2018-03-15 04:28] http404error: It's just too hard to bank on imo
[2018-03-15 04:29] http404error: I do like units that force your opponent to rethink their plan of course
[2018-03-15 04:29] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Cicada (1.3) allows DCi.
[2018-03-15 04:29] f300xen (f0x): i wonder how good a straight Cicada Cicada x4 opening is
[2018-03-15 04:29] http404error: I think vs that you just have to go blue first for wall
[2018-03-15 04:30] f300xen (f0x): what if you go second
[2018-03-15 04:30] http404error: But the buildtime makes the decision interesting though
[2018-03-15 04:30] supergreyman (Greyman): The original Savior was techless attacker.
[2018-03-15 04:31] supergreyman (Greyman): I never got to play with it unfortunately.
[2018-03-15 04:31] f300xen (f0x): savior wass a techless attacker, savior was worse than drone
[2018-03-15 04:31] f300xen (f0x): therefore all techless attackers are worse than drone
[2018-03-15 04:34] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Cicada (1.4): Supply 4. Cost: 2:Gold: 1:Energy:. :Fragile:2. :Time:4, pay 1:Gold: each :Time:. Turn: Get 2:Gold:, :Attack:.
[2018-03-15 04:36] f300xen (f0x): *just make it consume a freaking drone*
[2018-03-15 04:37] f300xen (f0x): also it sucks, there is no point where i ever buy that for anything

[2018-03-15 17:40] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Since each Player gets +.
[2018-03-15 17:40] http404error: That's not why, necessarily
[2018-03-15 17:40] http404error: Yes, having more defense is a strictly good thing
[2018-03-15 17:40] redzonex (Redzone): the cost is simply associated with buying the unit
[2018-03-15 17:41] http404error: Buying a unit is neither strictly good nor strictly bad
[2018-03-15 17:41] http404error: It should never be strictly better to buy a unit than to not
[2018-03-15 17:41] http404error: Paying costs is always strictly bad
[2018-03-15 17:41] http404error: Because having more resources, units, defense, or attack, is strictly good
[2018-03-15 17:42] http404error: Giving your opponent attack later is just another kind of cost you're paying, just like sacrificing a drone, or paying :Green:
[2018-03-15 17:42] http404error: The key to understanding SDR in Prismata is to know which things "dominate" and which do not
[2018-03-15 17:43] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): But +/+ is valid, +-/0 is like buying.
[2018-03-15 17:43] http404error: These choices can each be made independently - if you make a unit that causes higher lifespan to no longer dominate, that does not break domination for the other parts of the game.
[2018-03-15 17:43] http404error: That's what is meant by "there is no one rule"
[2018-03-15 17:43] http404error: @StrShPlyr I don't understand what you mean by that.
[2018-03-15 17:44] http404error: In many games like Hearthstone and Magic the Gathering, there is essentially *nothing* that strictly dominates - only things that are almost always better
[2018-03-15 17:45] http404error: But preserving the sanctity of "always strictly better" is something that many Prismata players consider important, because it means you never have to "play around" the exceptions
[2018-03-15 17:45] http404error: The game's decision space is complex enough as it is, frankly.
[2018-03-15 17:45] darkeisbein: I thought about what if there was a unit that upgrades your biggest absorb by one and this came out:
3 :Gold: :Blue: :Blue: :Green: , at the start of your turn construct a barrier,  :Lifespan: 5
[2018-03-15 17:47] http404error: Neither of those break SDR, if that's what you're worried about
[2018-03-15 17:48] http404error: It's like a slow granular Aegis... hmm
[2018-03-15 17:48] http404error: That's a really cool design
[2018-03-15 17:48] darkeisbein: it can also be played around by anything that plays around absorb
[2018-03-15 17:49] http404error: It accomplishes essentially the exact goal, but in a simpler more comprehensible way
[2018-03-15 17:49] http404error: Oh, I would think about having it make a Barrier when you buy it too
[2018-03-15 17:49] http404error: Just to make it easier to slot into a build
[2018-03-15 17:49] http404error: It's definitely very efficient as-is though
[2018-03-15 17:49] darkeisbein: I thought it's more interesting if you have to find a way to slot it in
[2018-03-15 17:49] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Prompt barrier's good.
[2018-03-15 17:50] darkeisbein: like big absorbers are not prompt more often then not
[2018-03-15 17:50] http404error: I do appreciate how you realized you could do basically the same thing as upgrading defense without having to introduce a new mechanic for it, though
[2018-03-15 17:50] darkeisbein: and I like it that way
[2018-03-15 17:50] http404error: A lot of designers miss those kinds of things
[2018-03-15 17:51] supergreyman (Greyman): One of the things I like about Prismata is that you could have a lot of various effects with only a few mechanics.
[2018-03-15 17:52] supergreyman (Greyman): So for example, when one asks, "How can I make AOE chill?" Well, just make a bunch of chill units like Endotherm. 😄

[2018-03-15 17:54] darkeisbein: true AoE breaks SDR anyways
[2018-03-15 17:54] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Contraption: Get Doomed Mech and Nivo Kit. If Mech is killed, destroy Nivo Kit.

[2018-03-15 17:56] darkeisbein: instead of this weird mess
[2018-03-15 17:57] redzonex (Redzone): also yeah you could make that into like a blocking vai mauronax

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: That's not why, necessarily

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Yes, having more defense is a strictly good thing

messages.author: redzonex
messages.content: the cost is simply associated with buying the unit

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Buying a unit is neither strictly good nor strictly bad

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: It should never be strictly better to buy a unit than to not

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Paying costs is always strictly bad

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Because having more resources, units, defense, or attack, is strictly good

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: Giving your opponent attack later is just another kind of cost you're paying, just like sacrificing a drone, or paying :Green:

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: The key to understanding SDR in Prismata is to know which things "dominate" and which do not

messages.author: strshplyr5046
messages.content: But +/+ is valid, +-/0 is like buying.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: These choices can each be made independently - if you make a unit that causes higher lifespan to no longer dominate, that does not break domination for the other parts of the game.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: That's what is meant by "there is no one rule"

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: @StrShPlyr I don't understand what you mean by that.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: In many games like Hearthstone and Magic the Gathering, there is essentially *nothing* that strictly dominates - only things that are almost always better

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: But preserving the sanctity of "always strictly better" is something that many Prismata players consider important, because it means you never have to "play around" the exceptions

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: The game's decision space is complex enough as it is, frankly.
